Terrible product. Has broken 3 times over the last 6 years with the usual overflow water fault and significant damage. Total repair cost has significantly exceeded the price of the machine [$1700 to date]. Even worse, the service department is entirely useless, and when they were last contacted about  a rotator which had seized and melted the plastic [no fire hazard here folks],  they claimed there was no authorized repair facilities in New York City, and back I should 'just jiggle it'.

Stay away from this bad product.

reviewText: CAUTION! I have a set of new Whrilpool Duet washer and dryer. Brackets are the proper shape but about 3/4 of an inch too short vertically to fit the pre drilled holes on the appliances. I consulted both manuals extensively prior to ordering this stacking set for this new washer & dryer. I had to drill holes in the upper edge of the washer above the intended factory drilled holes. In the long run these brackets may work but don't seem as secure as they would be if manufactured the proper length. I've run a few loads and so far I'm not impressed. Aside from that 12.00 is a lot for two small pieces of tin and some tape.

reviewText: This mullion works on a LMX28988 refrigerator model, from 2011, I believe.  I mention it because LG customer service could not confirm that this part ending in 103 would work--I think they specified a slightly different part number.  Has been in use for three months with no problems.  It was far more convenient to order this from amazon instead of the supplier that LG recommended.

reviewText:
I fixed my own freezer!
Wish I had known this info before I did it, as I would have saved about fifty bucks.

If you have a freezer that *isn't freezing*, it is often because it isn't defrosting. (The frost buildup blocks the airflow past the cooling fins.)

#1  The Defrost Timer ( Frigidaire 297318010 Defrost Timer) is VERY likely to give out eventually. It is just a mechanical timer, so it has a small motor that can go bad or its metal contacts can get fouled by the normal arcing that happens as it switches from refrigerate to defrost mode and back. This is super easy to get to and replace from the back of the refrigerator/freezer. It's about ten to fifteen bucks, so it's a cheap easy fix. (To diagnose, look for the little dial that you can turn. If you turn it, it will click and the freezer should turn off, then turn it just a little more and it should turn back on. If that happens, the contacts are fine. (If it doesn't turn the freezer off and on, then the contacts are fouled - so replace the timer.) If the contacts are fine, next mark a line on the dial and move it to the 12 o'clock position. Wait a few hours and come back and check it. If it is still in the 12 o'clock position, then the motor on the timer is dead, and you need to replace it. If however the timer has turned to, say, 2 o'clock, then the timer is still good and it is very likely that there is a different cause for the freezer to have stopped.

#2  This Thermostat ( Frigidaire 297318010 Defrost Timer ) is located inside the freezer, behind the bottom back panel, near the heat exhanger that looks like a radiator. (On mine, it was at the upper left corner of the heat exchanger.) The thermostat is fairly easy to get to, and you can replace it for about ten bucks. (Especially if it looks bloated or like it came apart at all, just replace it.) To get to it, remove the shelves, and unscrew the panel. Pull the dial for the temperature selector straight off, and unclip the temp selector from the panel. There will likely be a LOT of ice behind it, if you haven't already defrosted the unit.  If you still have to defrost it, unplug the freezer and place a fan inside of it (up on a couple boards to  keep it out of the melting water). Leave the door open and the fan going for a couple hours and the ice will all be gone, easily, except for the water all over the floor... I'd defrost it in the garage, or outside if possible.

#3  The defrost heater / heating element ( Frigidaire 216730700 Defrost Heater for Freezer ) is located under the heat exchanger, and is attached with some thin aluminum strips. It shouldn't really go bad, so I'd try replacing the above two items first. But if they don't fix it... well here's another forty or fifty bucks to throw at fixing the problem. Be careful with the aluminum strips that hold the defrost heater in place, and you can re-use them. (If they break, do NOT use a non-aluminum wire to hold it in place, or you could ruin your freezer!)

Other diagnostics:  (I haven't done these, but they came up in my searches and fixes.)
  If the fan doesn't run inside, that's a fairly obvious thing to replace, and looks to be pretty easy. Be sure to cycle the defrost timer to check it.
  If there is just not enough cooling, but there is no frost buildup and the fan works, then the temperature selector might be the problem.
  If the compressor doesn't run, there is a capacitor that I've seen come up as a replacement part.
  If nothing works at all - no lights, no fan, no compressor, then you might have had a power surge that blew out the computer board.

reviewText: This will be our third bag replacement. The bag itself does the trick and is handy for holding small items like others have mentioned, but do not expect it to last a long time. The downfall is the quality and the price. When you start to see it coming apart, its best to get rid of it ASAP and get another one if you desire, as the pieces will continue to fall off and will fall inside your dishwasher bottom. The only reason I am replacing it, is for the convienece of having it, not for the quality.

reviewText: This part fits perfectly in my sink drain. 3" in diameter. The ones they sell at Lowes or Depot do not fit sinks made for GE disposals, so this was good for that purpose. It appears to be a durable part that will last a long time I'm sure. My only contention with it, thus the two star deduction, is that the water doesn't flow through it well at all, as many other reviewers have also stated. Even with the faucet turned on to half flow, the water pools up in the sink and takes a long time to drain through this part, which is a pain if you have a bunch of crud in the water from rinsing dishes. There are little rubber skins between the grooves that go from the center hole to their individual small holes on the outer. Some of the little holes also have this rubber remnant. It's like a leftover little skin from the likely injection mold process used to make these. I spent over an hour with my xacto knife cutting away these little skins, and making the center hole and all the small holes a little bigger. The water flows better, however, it still pools up a little when I have the faucet turned on full. I don't want to trim more because I don't want to lose the shielding from grinding food bits in the disposal that this part is meant to provide. So I guess it's a happy medium. Perhaps a different cutout design would provide ample water flow while maintaining a good guard against flying bits while running the disposal. It's too bad because there doesn't seem to be any better options out there for the GE size drains.

Since the unit is no longer in production, and is showing its age, I don't expect to need another filter several years from now.

reviewText: DO NOT BUY!!!! The compressor stopped working after 3 years and the replacement compressor stopped working 2 years after that. It cost $200 to repair, plus loss of food and the inconvenience of living out of an ice cooler. The wait for a new part is not worth it. Their designated repair shop in my area was equally horrible as they forgot about my repair and the part ended up going to someone else, absolutely no communication with me, I had to remind them to do their job. I ended up waiting two months for a new part, I refuse to do it again. Beware! The positive reviews on other sites are reproduced from Danby's web site. I wish there had been more reviews when I purchased this fridge. I would have bought something else or taken out some cabinets to accommodate a regular size fridge.

reviewText:
The motor on my Duet Sport burned out after relatively light use and only two and a half years.  Of course that's long enough that the company won't cover the defective motor.  Plus, the unit has a design flaw which traps water and causes mold and mildew.  Whirlpool's customer service seemed neither surprised nor sympathetic when I called to arrange service and register a complaint.

Looking into Kenmore which I hear has a much better front load energy saving model.  I won't buy Whirlpool again.  Ever.

reviewText:
Hard time figuring out right size & color & company. Once I did, they are perfect!!! Not too long, & sturdy.
WD'd the prong it was to go on to ease slipping them on. Fast & just what I needed for a Kohler left side black basket drainer I'd had since late 80's. No way to get replacement caps from Kohler for the Basket's Prongs that support the basket.
Thick Plastic covering was broke where they sat, so they were rusting my porcelain sink. These tips solved the problem & look nice.
Do wish black was available, as the basket is black. Sink is light gray so these gray caps looks good. Ha, like meant to be.
I picked right company too!

reviewText:
I was a little skeptical when I ordered this item, but was truly over-the-top impressed thus far!  This was well packaged and included 3 additional scrub heads (all different strengths light to extra heavy duty) in addition to the brush that was attached.  The heads are very easily removed and replaced with a 45 degree turn and click.  Once you fill the handle with your favorite dish soap, you give the button a little press and the soap is dispensed into the head.
What I am so impressed with on this product is the quality.  I was expectiing it to feel light and cheap - but it is definitely NOT!  It feels good and sturdy in my hand and I would tell anyone that this a great buy! I was fortunate to try this item at a discount to give my opinion, and this is a big thumbs up in my book!!!

reviewText: This machine is horrible.  I've had it for a year now and while I like certain features, the bottom line is that it does NOT clean clothes.  Even a pair of delicate stockings that I would put on 'hand wash / delicate' cycle, come out smelling as though they were never washed!  One time I put the wash through 3 cycles (nullifying all the energy efficiency saving features) and the clothes still smelled as though they came out of the hamper as opposed to the washing machine! For the price, I think that Electrolux should do a formal recall.  This washing machine is a total lemon.  Dryer is ok...gets job done, but not so thrilled with it based on price either.

reviewText:
Ten years ago my husband and I built a new home and decided to purchase the best quality appliances that we could and have them built in by the cabinet maker. We decided on Miele as was recommended by the retailer, and in particular was the microwave (M65EG)that was installed complete with the surrounding framework. It is an outright lemon.
Up until a month ago we had to remove the unit five times for repairs and take it the Miele repair shop which is a 180klm round trip each time we drop it off and then collect it. Each time there was a different issue and cost involved. Now we have it back from the last trip two weeks ago, and guess what,it has died again.
We are now in a situation where we are debating as to whether getting another repair or dumping it rather than continuing to chase our tails. Will we buy another brand and pay for the original cabinet area to be revamped, buy another Miele microwave and hope that the surrounds fit the new model.
We sent a complaint to Miele Australia by email and the reply was at best, sympathetic. Not good enough at all.

reviewText:
It's a great refrigerator, I had to switch the doors' side from left opening to right-hand opening, and had a good friend do it for me, and between the two of us we managed to solve that problem on our own. The refrigerator is keeping items in the freezer perfectly cold, and after leaving the adjustment on high for 24 hours, as the directions said to do, it was freezing ice just fine in trays.
The delivery company was a problem, as it was six days late after the first delivery date was passed by with no explanation. I kept having to make calls to the trucking company to find out what was going on. They got it to me within ten days after I first ordered it from NYC to near the panhandle of Florida. Not too bad! The delivery men could not get the refrigerator through the door and insisted on leaving it outside, as they were getting behind on their delivery schedules. Within five minutes on my own, I took down a rail on my steps, then the door opened wide enough. I had to get the same friend to help me get it up my back steps with his dolly, which presented it's own problems when one of the steps broke. However, we managed to get it into the house, plugged it in and within five minutes my new refrigerator started the compressor. I love this Frigidaire, and it was the delivery company with the issues.
This new refrigerator is quite a bit larger than my old refrigerator, which I am very glad for, and it as easy to set up, after we got it in the house. Thank you Amazon!

reviewText: When the original W10295370 filter expired, we bought the new Everydrop replacement  from Whirlpool.  The water tasted horrible with the new filter.  We replaced the reservoir in the refrigerator and the supply line as well.  Still tasted bad.  Couldn't return the bad filter because 30 days went past (It took time to get the plumber out here and to order the parts).  So, I ordered another one thinking I did everything on my end so it must have been a rogue filter.  That one came and I installed and flushed and used it for about 2 weeks.  The water tastes horrible.  I returned that one right away.  There must be a problem with the newly branded Everydrop because the old blue filters worked just fine.  I'm out the cost of a filter, a plumber and parts.  I'm going to try and find the older Blue Filter to see if maybe when Whirlpool switched to Everydrop, there was some manufacturing problems with these newer ones.

reviewText:
We bought this machine unfortunately, about 1 years ago.
We have had many problems since we bought it, we called customer service they recommend something, we went to trip and come back after 2 month, when we use the dishwasher after 3 days I opened the door all of my dishes was wet, had spot and sometimes left detergent on the dishes. Three LG technicians came and saw the problems, finally they replace it with a new one. We had the same problems from the day one. Their technicians came and saw our problem again, they recommended that we should install water softener, use the vinegar,  jetdry and everything that you could thick of and we did all of it. Still the problems did not go away. Their technician came for the last time and they referred us to LG customer service to buy back the unit. But the customer service denied our case. I have to wash my dishes by hand. This product is awful. Don't buy LG at all and don't waste your money.

reviewText:
If you are single and live in an apartment without space for your own washer and dryer, this is the way to go. It works great to get your clothes clean, and with a little extra labor it is well worth it. I did a little math, it cost me about $10 to $15 a week at the laundromat, that is $50 to $60 a month. My total water bill is $12.00 a month. Do a load with this every other day or so and save at least $40 a month. That adds up to $480.00 a year. That's not including the gas you burn driving there.

Things I've been able to wash with Wonderwash: a bunch of various whites, socks, underwear, a couple of T-shirts; a pair of jeans with a sweatshirt, 2 unlined curtain panels, and even a winged back chair cover.

reviewText:
This does what it is supposed to do.  I searched far and wide before finding this to allow my wife to turn on and off some medical equipment without having to get up.  It works great for that.  Unfortunately for me, right after finding this, and having not been able to find anything else before finding this, remote outlet setups have become very common and cost a lot less than this did.

The reason for the four stars is that it doesn't come with the A23 battery that it requires and nothing in the instructions tell you what battery is required.  The only way I found it was by the reviews here.  That battery is unusual and can be hard to find.  Local stores have one peg for them and that peg is empty more often than it has product.  Make sure you order the battery from Amazon when you order the switch.

reviewText:
VERY NICE PIECE OF EQUIPMENT FOR THE KITCHEN.  AND I'D GIVE IT 5 STARS EXCEPT THE INSTALLATION IS A BIT OF A NUISANCE.  THE MOUNTS ARE SO DIFFICULT TO GET TO WHEN INSTALLING THAT I THOUGHT IT WAS A JOKE AT FIRST.  i WOUND UP PUTTING TWO STRIPS OF WOOD ON THE SIDES OF THE EXISTING CABINETS TO ALLOW ME TO SUPPORT THIS AS THE 4 SCREWS THAT ARE DESIGNED TO HOLD IT IN PLACE ARE COMPLETELY INADEQUATE.  ONCE INSTALLED, THE UNIT FUNCTIONS PERFECTLY.  I DID FIND ONE SMALL PLASTIC CLIP IN THE BOTTOM OF THE DRAWER THAT LOCKS ONE OF THE PLATE BRACKETS IN THE UPRIGHT POSITION WHEN NEEDED.  IT WAS BROKEN OFF.  I CONTACTED THE MFG WHO IS SENDING ME A REPLACEMENT.  VERY EASY TO USE.  VERY EFFICIENT USING MUCH LESS WATER THAN CONVENTIONAL DESIGNED D/W'S.  MUCH EASIER TO LOAD/UNLOAD AS IT SITS HIGHER THEN THE BACK LOWER DRAWER ON CONVENTIONAL UNITS.  THERE IS EVEN A RED LIGHT INSIDE ON THE HANDLE OF THE JET DRY DISPENSER TO LET YOU KNOW YOU NEED TO REFILL WITH MORE.  VERY HIGH END UNIT.

I wanted to add this after 6 month use:  Absolutely the best.  We discovered that the unit actually steams the dishes after loading even if you don't turn it right on immediately thus softening the debris and keeping it soft until the cycle is started.  We found this out by accident when after loading and forgetting to turn it on the same day we found the dishes had moisture and the debris could actually be washed off in the sink under water.  Just a real well thought out piece of kitchen equipment all the way.
summary: HIGH QUALITY, EFFICIENT UNIT WITH DIFFICULT INSTALLATION PROCESS.

high quality, efficient unit with difficult installation process.. very nice piece of equipment for the kitchen.  and i'd give it 5 stars except the installation is a bit of a nuisance.  the mounts are so difficult to get to when installing that i thought it was a joke at first.  i wound up putting two strips of wood on the sides of the existing cabinets to allow me to support this as the 4 screws that are designed to hold it in place are completely inadequate.  once installed, the unit functions perfectly.  i did find one small plastic clip in the bottom of the drawer that locks one of the plate brackets in the upright position when needed.  it was broken off.  i contacted the mfg who is sending me a replacement.  very easy to use.  very efficient using much less water than conventional designed d/w's.  much easier to load/unload as it sits higher then the back lower drawer on conventional units.  there is even a red light inside on the handle of the jet dry dispenser to let you know you need to refill with more.  very high end unit.

i wanted to add this after 6 month use:  absolutely the best.  we discovered that the unit actually steams the dishes after loading even if you don't turn it right on immediately thus softening the debris and keeping it soft until the cycle is started.  we found this out by accident when after loading and forgetting to turn it on the same day we found the dishes had moisture and the debris could actually be washed off in the sink under water.  just a real well thought out piece of kitchen equipment all the way.

reviewText: Reviews here indicated that the unit has a high failure rate, so I got the insurance from Asurion for $11.99.  The ice maker worked for 15 months and then the "Ice Full" sensor failed.  (The "Add Water" sensor never worked, and I suspect that reports of the motor burning out quickly were due to customers not realizing that the water reservoir was empty, so we got into the habit of monitoring that.)  The claim through Asurion was easy, so we got to use the ice maker for more than a year at a cost of $11.99.  If I can't find a better option, we'll get the same kind and play the same game again.  The cubes are on the small side and they melt quickly while sitting in the reservoir; not sure what could be done about that, but then I'm not an engineer.  It would be nice to be able to see how far the unit is through a cycle, so that you can time better when to turn it off.

Bought this in Feb 2016. Was successfuly able to use it venting to the indoors for about 12 months and then had a contractor come in and he was able to set it up to exhaust outside. I was happy with that.

The issue of the buttons that other people have mentioned is the reason I'm writing this review. The highest speed exhaust button is now lost inside the hood and I cannot retrieve it or find a way to order a replacement. Using a screwdriver to make it work, which stinks. Bummed that we just really set it up to work at capacity and now we still have to fix something about it. Tried to call customer service and was told they were in a training and the phone hung up on me - no mention of when to call back to option to leave my # for a call. Wouldn't buy this again at this point...

reviewText:
This thing saved me having to buy a new freezer.

I had an inch of frost on my upright freezer and thought that I was going to have to buy a new unit because it couldn't hold a low temperature. Since it was soon-too-be trash anyway, I watched a few YouTube videos and decided to take a stab at fixing my freezer.

First, I left it unplugged and defrosted it. Then, I unscrewed all of the obvious panels and got out a volt meter for some testing. When I plugged in, nothing on the defroster was hot, which had me suspicious, but I had never done this before so I took a screwdriver and advanced the factory timer a few notches and re-tested for a few spins. Dead.

I then took out the timer and tested a pass-through current. Hot.

Okay, I just need a new timer. I ordered this one, got in in two days, plugged it in, secured it, and now have a working freezer for about 10% of the cost of a new one.

Test first and make sure you need the thing. If you do, this unit will be fine as long as the freezer model numbers match.

But now the outdoor sensor doesn't work at all, despite the fact that it is protected from the weather. We live in a pretty wet climate, so the battery terminals on the outdoor sensor continually get corroded. But even though we clean them regularly, the unit simply fails to function. I encourage you to shop for another brand.

reviewText: I have a Kenmore HE4. Had the SUDS message on my display. This pump does work. However, the more wash cycles we did, the noise of the pump increased. It was already very loud when first installed, but got louder and louder the more cycles we did. I decided to take the pump off and have a look. Well, water had gotten past the impeller shaft and had filled the pump with water. I don't think that is where water is supposed to be. This is not an oem part and would recommend spending the $150 for a genuine part, even though it comes with the other parts you don't need. I bought the OEM part, installed it, and now the washer is just as quiet as it was when new.  One thing to note, if you have SUDS on your display, make sure there is nothing stuck in the drain (the one behind the bottom panel). I didn't think there was cause I opened the clean out plug and didn't see anything, BUT make sure to take the black rubber drain off the back of the clean out plug housing to look in there. I found part of a bra stuck in there which may have been causing the Suds message all along .

reviewText: I bought this product because I needed to monitor my home temperature readings while I was away on vacation. I was initially skeptical about this product after reading the reviews and the problems with the website and people not being able to access the sensor readings online. I have had nothing but great performance from this product and it has done exactly what I needed it to do while I was away. I've had this product for a little over a month now and just got back from vacation and never had problems accessing the sensors or the website to see the readings. I was using the Acu-Link mobile app from my phone the whole time on a slow 3G connection and it still managed to get the readings just fine but it does take a while for the readings to come up. The communication does work 10 times better if your using a wi-fi or high speed data connection. I'm now planning on purchasing the more advanced sensor that gets wind, rain, and temperature readings. I Highly reccomend this product!

reviewText: Installed this filter in my GE Profile french door refrigerator,  then the ice make stopped working! I thought I would need to replace the icemaker, or worse, the refrigerator. Paid a GE service tech $$ to troubleshoot: It is this filter causing my icemaker to fail! I removed the filter, inserted the filter plug to see if the icemaker would make ice, and sure enough, it does! I ordered a GE OEM filter for $40 on Amazon. Spend the extra money on buying OEM GE filter!!! This filter can damage your expensive refrigerator!

reviewText:
You'll think it doesn't fit. You'll measure your drive pulley and the belt (roughly 38 in) and realize there is about a 2 in difference. You'll be positive they messed up. You will go through a range of emotions. I did. You will watch How To videos and see how easily theirs pops on if you turn it hard enough and you'll think yours can't possibly do this because it doesn't even go half way around and tightens underneath. You'll curse those How To videos.

Here's what I did. I hope it doesn't break anything of yours. I wet it and microwaved it 10 to15 sec increments (I had no other heating method) and would stretch with gloves or pulled from my shoes. I then heated it and put it on immediately _ with 3 zip ties close together. Then turned it as in the videos on you tube being careful not to turn on the side it needs to pop on. I thought it might break but I turned harder (just from pure anger) and it did pop on.

I immediately put on Rocky themed music and paraded around while my children joined in. We were not in rhythm but we were joyous and our clothes soon would be clean. Hope this helps.

Months later they are still giving me the run around, making me reverse door plates and so-on, like I'm a refrigerator repairman.  There is nothing special about this fridge, I bought because it matched my grill.  IMO you are likely to do better with a different brand unless you are a really good refrigerator mechanic or enjoy spending time on the phone.

reviewText:
This facilitated a straightforward repair of my Maytag Atlantis MAV6200AWQ washing machine, which had suddenly developed a bad bearing noise during the agitate and spin cycles.  Tip machine forward to remove belt from bottom, pop off top panel (a flat implement can be inserted between top and front panel to disengage two retaining clips) and remove two screws at top corners of front panel to access pump.  Remove hoses (have bucket ready), remove three screws holding pump, swap parts and reverse steps.  Cheap and easy!

UPDATE: The bearing failed after one year (1-2 normal loads weekly).  The seller was unresponsive when I requested guidance for replacement under the advertised GARP lifetime warranty, so I bought another one from a different seller, which failed after one year as well.  The second seller indicated that the manufacturer now is out of business, and as reviewers of other GARP products have pointed out, the warranty address ("garparts.co/warranty") references a domain that does not exist, so buyer beware!

reviewText: I have only had this machine for 7 months now and I have been having major problems with it for the last two months. Secondly,  I am being told that I will have to conduct the repairs myself,  they will send the necessary parts based on their assessment of the problem. The other  option is to ship the unit back to the nearest store which is in Canada. Bear in mind that shipping is at your expense, both ways!! Add $180! Unless you are handy with such repairs, which I am not, my suggestion is to purchase a product that provides repairs locally, within the contiguous US, at the very least!

reviewText:
I received a free item to review.

And I cant think of a more accurate description: the ice cubes are shaped like hollowed-out rounded cones, not the semi-circular solids many refrigerator built-ins produce nor the classic cube shape you get with plastic trays in the freezer (I can remember when the trays were aluminum, with a pull-up release lever).

Ive had these cone-shaped cubes with drinks before and one advantage is theres less displacement in the glass so you get more of what you're drinking. And some people enjoy the mini-frosted-mug effect. The downside is if you enjoy sucking on, sloshing around, and biting down on your ice cubes, the cone shape tends to break down quickly as the rounded walls thin down.

It takes just over a 6-cup Brita pitcher-full of water to fill the icemaker to the max indicator  its nice that the indicator doubles as a ledge for the cube basket and is easy to see rather than a raised line I have to squint or feel around for. The compartment lid stays open by itself so pouring is comfortable.

Its very easy to operate  the start/standby button displays either a solid snowflake icon (standby) or a blinking one (running). Select from three sizes of cubes that are noticeably different (although all are cone-shaped). Theres a Set button for the clock and auto-on/off timer function you program similarly to digital clocks on stereos and microwaves.

Unfortunately the buttons are press tabs rather than actual buttons, but they protrude enough that the response is adequate.

Youll have to read the instruction page to identify the other warning icons and to troubleshoot: low water, jammed cube, etc. Theyre not intuitive and not terribly easy to see with the lit-LCD screen facing straight out, but I didn't encounter any if the handful of times Ive used the unit so far.

Speaking of facing, of the three icemaker models Ivation offers in this class, this one closely resembles our Japanese rice cooker in shape and color and makes for a nice pairing on our storage island.

That said the ice maker is quite big sitting on our countertop  larger than our rice cooker and deeper than our coffeemaker. Its the kind of unit wed expect to haul out when entertaining, while we rely on our refrigerators built-in for everyday crushed ice and small cubes.

It took about two hours to make medium and small-sized cubes using just about all of the water Id poured in. The unit will detect and go into standby when the basket is full so theres no worry about overflow. I emptied the basket twice, the first time at about the one hour mark.

One thing to be aware of: changing cube sizes in the middle of a run (after youve emptied the basket and resuming with water still in the unit) doesnt take effect immediately.  With about half the water remaining I still got mostly medium-sized cubes before the small-size finally kicked in.

The drain plug is positioned in the back lower right side corner and its easy to tilt the ice maker over the edge of a sink and twist the cap off  and pull out the little push seal.  I suppose that makes for a more secure seal with the unit typically on a countertop, but theres no mention in the instructions and it took awhile to figure out why the water wouldnt draining! An extra push seal is included underneath the basket along with the cube shovel.

The noise of the compressor is slightly less than what I hear with the refrigerator door open. Theres no detectable vibration while the unit operates. The intermittent sounds are water flowing into the evaporator chamber and the cubes themselves clanking into the basket.

Bottom Line: The ice maker is well-made and attractive  though too large to keep permanently on our (not so large countertop). The hollow cone-shaped cubes are an acquired taste but if you entertain a lot and need to crank out more ice than the fridge can handle (or you don't have a built-in), its a handy and easy-to-use addition.

Unfortunately the buttons are press tabs rather than actual buttons, but they protrude enough that the response is adequate.

Youll have to read the instruction page to identify the other warning icons and to troubleshoot: low water, jammed cube, etc. Theyre not intuitive and not terribly easy to see with the lit-LCD screen facing straight out, but I didn't encounter any if the handful of times Ive used the unit so far.

Speaking of facing, of the three icemaker models Ivation offers in this class, this one closely resembles our Japanese rice cooker in shape and color and makes for a nice pairing on our storage island.

That said the ice maker is quite big sitting on our countertop  larger than our rice cooker and deeper than our coffeemaker. Its the kind of unit wed expect to haul out when entertaining, while we rely on our refrigerators built-in for everyday crushed ice and small cubes.

It took about two hours to make medium and small-sized cubes using just about all of the water Id poured in. The unit will detect and go into standby when the basket is full so theres no worry about overflow. I emptied the basket twice, the first time at about the one hour mark.

One thing to be aware of: changing cube sizes in the middle of a run (after youve emptied the basket and resuming with water still in the unit) doesnt take effect immediately.  With about half the water remaining I still got mostly medium-sized cubes before the small-size finally kicked in.

The drain plug is positioned in the back lower right side corner and its easy to tilt the ice maker over the edge of a sink and twist the cap off  and pull out the little push seal.  I suppose that makes for a more secure seal with the unit typically on a countertop, but theres no mention in the instructions and it took awhile to figure out why the water wouldnt draining! An extra push seal is included underneath the basket along with the cube shovel.

The noise of the compressor is slightly less than what I hear with the refrigerator door open. Theres no detectable vibration while the unit operates. The intermittent sounds are water flowing into the evaporator chamber and the cubes themselves clanking into the basket.

Bottom Line: The ice maker is well-made and attractive  though too large to keep permanently on our (not so large countertop). The hollow cone-shaped cubes are an acquired taste but if you entertain a lot and need to crank out more ice than the fridge can handle (or you don't have a built-in), its a handy and easy-to-use addition.

reviewText: I use Cascade complete pods with this and my dishes come out sparkling every time. I've put extremely dirty dishes in it, without rinsing, just to see if it could handle it. Good news - it can! It also holds more dishes than expected, if you're only using plates. My plates are about 11inches square, and they fit no problem.  The rack isn't very well designed for bowls though. The spikes aren't quite wide enough, so bowls take up more room than they should. It can also be difficult to load large dishes in it, because the rack doesn't pull out far enough to clear the top of the inside of the dishwasher. This is really a minor problem though. I would recommend this dishwasher to anyone.

reviewText: I ordered this Avanti compact refrigerator (AR4596SS) on Wednesday, May 7, 2014. Since I have Amazon Prime, I expected it to arrive on Friday. And Friday morning, the tracking on Amazon said it was "Out for Delivery." I needed to take it up a flight of stairs, so I had a helper standing by to help me get it into my home. By 6pm on Friday it had not arrived. So I called Amazon. After waiting on hold for several minutes, I was told it was on its way and would arrive in an hour. Well, 7pm came and went, and it still did not arrive. So I called Amazon again. This time I was told that it was never even scheduled for delivery on Friday; that it would be coming on Saturday. So I asked, "Why did the Web site say it was 'Our for delivery' and why was I was told at 6pm that it was coming in an hour?" All the Amazon representative could say was that that was the info the site administrator and the previous rep had, but she now had the correct info. If it had just been me I wouldn't have been too upset, but as I said, I had another person waiting to help me. So we both wasted the whole day waiting for a package that wasn't even scheduled to arrive until the next day. In addition, before I ordered it, I checked the weather, as I knew I'd be outside for a while hooking the fridge up to a dolly. Friday the weather was to be nice, but storms were forecast for Saturday. That is why I ordered it on Wednesday, so I'd get it on Friday. I didn't want to be outside on Saturday in the middle of a lightening storm messing with a metal fridge and dolly. The forecast was right: it was nice on Friday and stormed on Saturday. But the fridge did not come until 5pm on Saturday, so the storms had passed by then. But it was two wasted days for me and my helper. However, for all of the hassles, Amazon refunded a significant portion of the cost. So for that I am thankful, and it is why Amazon is my "go-to" place for Internet shopping. Their service is usually very good, and if they do screw something up, they usually make it good somehow. But be forewarned when ordering large items, as there could be hassles with the shipping. That said, on the fridge itself, the reason I was getting a compact fridge was for "overflow" from my main fridge. Specifically, I try to eat mostly organic food, especially fruits and veggies. The best place to get such in the Pittsburgh area where I live is Pittsburgh's "East End Food Co-op." But that is over an half hour drive for me, so I only go once a month. As such, I really need to stock-up when I go. A compact fridge enables me to get even more fresh produce and not have it spoil by having it in a fridge the whole time. I had a compact fridge before this one, but it had a freezer compartment. As soon s I got that fridge, I knew it was a mistake. The freezer compartment was worthless. The only thing it would freeze was water. Even spaghetti sauce or chicken juice did not freeze. But what it did do was get covered with ice very quickly, so I had to defrost the freezer every month or two. Doing so required taking everything out of the fridge and freezer compartment, putting a tray under the freezer compartment to catch the melting ice, then wiping up the water that overflowed the tray, and then putting everything back in. It was a real pain, so I got rid of it. Unless you have a pressing need to make ice, do not get a compact fridge with a freezer compartment. That is why I got this Avanti fridge, as it does not have a freezer compartment. In any case, I finally got the fridge into my home and set up where I wanted it. The front feet are adjustable, so I adjusted them as needed. But I read somewhere in researching compact fridges that you should let a fridge sit for several hours after moving it before plugging it in. I'm not exactly sure why, but it has something to do with letting the cooling elements settle. So not wanting to damage my new fridge, I let it sit overnight. The next morning, I plugged it in and put a fridge thermometer in it. But when I went to turn it on and adjust the temperature, I noticed a minor problem with this fridge. The inside is white, including the knob for adjusting the temperature (located on the right hand side in about the middle of the fridge), but the numbering on the knob is light gold, so it is barely readable. I had to use a magnifying glass to read the numbers. But with doing so, I set it to the middle and let it cool. It only took about an hour. And I've only had to adjust the temperature a couple of times since then, so the hard to read numbering on the knob is not too much of a problem. But the second problem is much more significant. After I got everything in it with no problems, I put a two quart jug of reconstituted protein powder on the door and shut it. But when I went to open the door later, it was stuck! I couldn't open it. I had to shake it to get it opened, which with the "not moving it after it is plugged in advice" was probably not a good idea, but it was the only way I could get it opened. After some investigation I figured out what happened. The jug was "so heavy" it bent the door down, so it was catching on the bottom of the fridge. I had to pull up rather strongly on the door to straiten it up, which made me uncomfortable, as I was afraid I would break it. I didn't, but I knew then that I could not put anything "heavy" on the door. But even with only putting a few very light items on the door, the door still sticks. So I need to gently pull up on the door each and every time I open and close it. Since there is no handle on the door, this is not easy to do. I have to use both hands and grab onto the little grip at the top of the door with my fingertips to open and close it. This is a real pain, especially when I am putting something into the fridge. Rather than holding the item in hand and opening the fridge with the other as anyone reading this has probably done thousands of times without thinking about it, I have to set the item down, open the door with both hands, then hold the door open with one hand as I pick up the item with the other hand and then put it into the fridge. Because this is something that irritates me every time I open and close the fridge, I am only giving it three stars. But that is shame, as if it wasn't for that one problem, I would probably give this fridge five stars. As indicated, I got this fridge in May; it is now October. So I've had it for five months, and it has been working fine that whole time. The fridge stays cool, even when I load it up after my monthly Co-op trip, so it is a reliable fridge. Plus, there is a lot of space for a compact fridge, so I can really stock up on healthy foods. And eating lots of organic produce and other healthy foods has improved my health. That is no surprise, as I discuss the benefits of doing so in my God-given Foods Eating Plan book. But when I was between compact fridges, I wasn't going to the Co-op, so I wasn't eating quite as healthy as I am now. Bottom line: this Avanti fridge is reliable and roomy, and the lack of a freezer compartment is a big plus. If they would just fix the door and use black numbers on the temperature knob, it would be ideal.

After waiting on hold for several minutes, I was told it was on its way and would arrive in an hour. Well, 7pm came and went, and it still did not arrive. So I called Amazon again. This time I was told that it was never even scheduled for delivery on Friday; that it would be coming on Saturday. So I asked, "Why did the Web site say it was 'Our for delivery' and why was I was told at 6pm that it was coming in an hour?" All the Amazon representative could say was that that was the info the site administrator and the previous rep had, but she now had the correct info. If it had just been me I wouldn't have been too upset, but as I said, I had another person waiting to help me. So we both wasted the whole day waiting for a package that wasn't even scheduled to arrive until the next day. In addition, before I ordered it, I checked the weather, as I knew I'd be outside for a while hooking the fridge up to a dolly. Friday the weather was to be nice, but storms were forecast for Saturday. That is why I ordered it on Wednesday, so I'd get it on Friday. I didn't want to be outside on Saturday in the middle of a lightening storm messing with a metal fridge and dolly. The forecast was right: it was nice on Friday and stormed on Saturday. But the fridge did not come until 5pm on Saturday, so the storms had passed by then. But it was two wasted days for me and my helper. However, for all of the hassles, Amazon refunded a significant portion of the cost. So for that I am thankful, and it is why Amazon is my "go-to" place for Internet shopping. Their service is usually very good, and if they do screw something up, they usually make it good somehow. But be forewarned when ordering large items, as there could be hassles with the shipping. That said, on the fridge itself, the reason I was getting a compact fridge was for "overflow" from my main fridge. Specifically, I try to eat mostly organic food, especially fruits and veggies. The best place to get such in the Pittsburgh area where I live is Pittsburgh's "East End Food Co-op." But that is over an half hour drive for me, so I only go once a month. As such, I really need to stock-up when I go. A compact fridge enables me to get even more fresh produce and not have it spoil by having it in a fridge the whole time. I had a compact fridge before this one, but it had a freezer compartment. As soon s I got that fridge, I knew it was a mistake. The freezer compartment was worthless. The only thing it would freeze was water. Even spaghetti sauce or chicken juice did not freeze. But what it did do was get covered with ice very quickly, so I had to defrost the freezer every month or two. Doing so required taking everything out of the fridge and freezer compartment, putting a tray under the freezer compartment to catch the melting ice, then wiping up the water that overflowed the tray, and then putting everything back in. It was a real pain, so I got rid of it. Unless you have a pressing need to make ice, do not get a compact fridge with a freezer compartment. That is why I got this Avanti fridge, as it does not have a freezer compartment. In any case, I finally got the fridge into my home and set up where I wanted it. The front feet are adjustable, so I adjusted them as needed. But I read somewhere in researching compact fridges that you should let a fridge sit for several hours after moving it before plugging it in. I'm not exactly sure why, but it has something to do with letting the cooling elements settle. So not wanting to damage my new fridge, I let it sit overnight. The next morning, I plugged it in and put a fridge thermometer in it. But when I went to turn it on and adjust the temperature, I noticed a minor problem with this fridge. The inside is white, including the knob for adjusting the temperature (located on the right hand side in about the middle of the fridge), but the numbering on the knob is light gold, so it is barely readable. I had to use a magnifying glass to read the numbers. But with doing so, I set it to the middle and let it cool. It only took about an hour. And I've only had to adjust the temperature a couple of times since then, so the hard to read numbering on the knob is not too much of a problem. But the second problem is much more significant. After I got everything in it with no problems, I put a two quart jug of reconstituted protein powder on the door and shut it. But when I went to open the door later, it was stuck! I couldn't open it. I had to shake it to get it opened, which with the "not moving it after it is plugged in advice" was probably not a good idea, but it was the only way I could get it opened. After some investigation I figured out what happened. The jug was "so heavy" it bent the door down, so it was catching on the bottom of the fridge. I had to pull up rather strongly on the door to straiten it up, which made me uncomfortable, as I was afraid I would break it. I didn't, but I knew then that I could not put anything "heavy" on the door. But even with only putting a few very light items on the door, the door still sticks. So I need to gently pull up on the door each and every time I open and close it. Since there is no handle on the door, this is not easy to do. I have to use both hands and grab onto the little grip at the top of the door with my fingertips to open and close it. This is a real pain, especially when I am putting something into the fridge. Rather than holding the item in hand and opening the fridge with the other as anyone reading this has probably done thousands of times without thinking about it, I have to set the item down, open the door with both hands, then hold the door open with one hand as I pick up the item with the other hand and then put it into the fridge. Because this is something that irritates me every time I open and close the fridge, I am only giving it three stars. But that is shame, as if it wasn't for that one problem, I would probably give this fridge five stars. As indicated, I got this fridge in May; it is now October. So I've had it for five months, and it has been working fine that whole time. The fridge stays cool, even when I load it up after my monthly Co-op trip, so it is a reliable fridge. Plus, there is a lot of space for a compact fridge, so I can really stock up on healthy foods. And eating lots of organic produce and other healthy foods has improved my health. That is no surprise, as I discuss the benefits of doing so in my God-given Foods Eating Plan book. But when I was between compact fridges, I wasn't going to the Co-op, so I wasn't eating quite as healthy as I am now. Bottom line: this Avanti fridge is reliable and roomy, and the lack of a freezer compartment is a big plus. If they would just fix the door and use black numbers on the temperature knob, it would be ideal.

i had a compact fridge before this one, but it had a freezer compartment. as soon s i got that fridge, i knew it was a mistake. the freezer compartment was worthless. the only thing it would freeze was water. even spaghetti sauce or chicken juice did not freeze. but what it did do was get covered with ice very quickly, so i had to defrost the freezer every month or two. doing so required taking everything out of the fridge and freezer compartment, putting a tray under the freezer compartment to catch the melting ice, then wiping up the water that overflowed the tray, and then putting everything back in. it was a real pain, so i got rid of it. unless you have a pressing need to make ice, do not get a compact fridge with a freezer compartment. that is why i got this avanti fridge, as it does not have a freezer compartment.

in any case, i finally got the fridge into my home and set up where i wanted it. the front feet are adjustable, so i adjusted them as needed. but i read somewhere in researching compact fridges that you should let a fridge sit for several hours after moving it before plugging it in. i'm not exactly sure why, but it has something to do with letting the cooling elements settle. so not wanting to damage my new fridge, i let it sit overnight. the next morning, i plugged it in and put a fridge thermometer in it. but when i went to turn it on and adjust the temperature, i noticed a minor problem with this fridge. the inside is white, including the knob for adjusting the temperature (located on the right hand side in about the middle of the fridge), but the numbering on the knob is light gold, so it is barely readable. i had to use a magnifying glass to read the numbers. but with doing so, i set it to the middle and  let it cool. it only took about an hour. and i've only had to adjust the temperature a couple of times since then, so the hard to read numbering on the knob is not too much of a problem.

but even with only putting a few very light items on the door, the door still sticks. so i need to gently pull up on the door each and every time i open and close it. since there is no handle on the door, this is not easy to do. i have to use both hands and grab onto the little grip at the top of the door with my fingertips to open and close it. this is a real pain, especially when i am putting something into the fridge. rather than holding the item in hand and opening the fridge with the other as anyone reading this has probably done thousands of times without thinking about it, i have to set the item down, open the door with both hands, then hold the door open with one hand as i pick up the item with the other hand and then put it into the fridge.

because this is something that irritates me every time i open and close the fridge, i am only giving it three stars. but that is shame, as if it wasn't for that one problem, i would probably give this fridge five stars. as indicated, i got this fridge in may; it is now october. so i've had it for five months, and it has been working fine that whole time. the fridge stays cool, even when i load it up after my monthly co-op trip, so it is a reliable fridge. plus, there is a lot of space for a compact fridge, so i can really stock up on healthy foods. and eating lots of organic produce and other healthy foods has improved my health. that is no surprise, as i discuss the benefits of doing so in my god-given foods eating plan book. but when i was between compact fridges, i wasn't going to the co-op, so i wasn't eating quite as healthy as i am now.

bottom line: this avanti fridge is reliable and roomy, and the lack of a freezer compartment is a big plus. if they would just fix the door and use black numbers on the temperature knob, it would be ideal.

reviewText:
My city water is not that bad just a little chlorine taste if any.
This filter does the job making that taste go completely away only downside that its costly.
I would recommend this only if its on sale .
I no longer use this ,but i still have the GE Fridge.

I bought the plug by pass which you take the filter off and place the plug in place where the filter goes.
It screws in just like the filter except it does not filter anything it lets the water flow through.
And I bought an external filter kit that goes between the water line that connects to the fridge.
Basically the line that goes to my fridge connects to my water from under Kitchen faucet sink.
That is where I put the external filter ,but you can place it anywhere.
This external filter is far cheaper and far better and lasts allot longer and many more respected brands to choose from,;)

reviewText:
Did not buy through Amazon, so they're off the hook.  This unit has been in my house for 6 months and after one failed service call and one cancelled call by the service company, it still does not work properly.  The oven temperature controls are erratic at best.  Takes 35 minutes to heat to 350.  The temperature control band is somewhere in the range of +20 to - 70 degrees from the setting.  We monitor with a remote reading temperature probe, so as not to be influenced by opening the door.  Have set the oven temperature to 450 and watched the actual temperature exceed 500 before we shut it down.  Neither oven will properly control temperature.  Don't buy this if you even think about baking anything.
The range burners work fine.  Nice heavy cast iron grates, reasonable heat output for a home unit.
summary: Do Not Buy if you Bake

The oven temperature controls are erratic at best.  Takes 35 minutes to heat to 350.  The temperature control band is somewhere in the range of +20 to - 70 degrees from the setting.  We monitor with a remote reading temperature probe, so as not to be influenced by opening the door.  Have set the oven temperature to 450 and watched the actual temperature exceed 500 before we shut it down.  Neither oven will properly control temperature.  Don't buy this if you even think about baking anything.
The range burners work fine.  Nice heavy cast iron grates, reasonable heat output for a home unit.

reviewText: Dont buy this hood! About 8 months in, the hood started to mysteriously turn off and not turn back on until the next day or so. And now, it won't turn on at all. Customer service is a joke. They keep telling me that they are helpful without actually being so. It doesn't help to tell me they never have problems with their hoods when I am currently having a problem! All they can do is send me parts but I can't even tell which part is the problem. My handyman checked the power source at various points and the dang thing should be working -- but it's not. Which means we are now in a game of guessing/deduction. Oh, and they have no record of my purchase (I have the bank statement). Not fun and much stress. Meanwhile, I have an attractive but functionless appliance in my kitchen.

reviewText: Wow! I've never cracked open an appliance to fix it before so I was apprehensive about this. When I read how easy it was and thought about the savings, I had to try. The door of my Amana ADB1500AWS had been sticking for a while. This week it finally stuck shut for good, and I had to use a small flathead screwdriver slid in under the handle in order to force the latch by pushing from the outside. Then it was broken for real. Well, buying this latch assembly was worth it-- it was a total success! Here are the steps I took... 1. I got my supplies together. I ordered this and DEWALT DW2067 Torx Insert Bit Set, 7-Piece for my ratcheting screwdriver. I ordered them using 2-day shipping, but I actually received the stuff in just one day. 2. With the T20 torx bit in my ratcheting screwdriver, I opened the dishwasher door and removed all 11 torx screws from the inside perimeter of the door, including the top center two that held the latch assembly in place. I washed the screws and the inside of the door under the screw locations because they were dusty, and then I removed the door's inside plastic panel and set it to the side. The inner door panel was connected by wires-- I didn't disconnect them, so the inner panel was attached the whole time even though the panel was set to the side. 3. The old latch assembly had only been attached by the door's two center screws that I'd already removed and two pairs of wire plugs. Without tools, I pulled out the latch assembly including the door handle and disconnected the wire plugs. 4. I plugged in the new latch assembly the way the previous one had been connected, and dropped it into its place, handle first. 5. I returned the inner door to its correct spot and put the screws back in tightly. 6. I closed the dishwasher door and appreciated the assured "click" and the door's satisfying ability to open by a smooth, easy motion of the handle. I closed the door and began a cycle to make sure it would start. I opened the door after the cycle started to make sure the dishwasher would pause. Everything worked perfectly. This was a big win for me-- a money saver, a convenient-easy-practical fix, and confidence-builder, too! It would have probably taken more time to even get a repair-person out here for the initial visit!

reviewText: Love this machine!  I was unsure of buying a gizmo like this, but after lots of research and finally clicking the purchase button, I took a leap of faith, and could not be any happier with my decision.  Not only was it delivered a day before expected (purchased from First&Best), it exceeded my expectations as far as size, quietness, maneuverability, capacity, and overall ability to clean my laundry.  I seriously saved my laundry, since I knew my machine would be arriving, and did it all on the day it arrived on my door step.  As many others, the hose provided to attach to your sink is not a match to my faucet.  However, I am storing my machine in the bathroom and when I use it, I simply set it next to the bathtub and use the hand held shower head to fill the machine with water....-Perfection!  (and no having to pick up any kind of heavy buckets filled with water!).  I am amazed at how simple, functional, practical, and inexpensive this machine is.  I could really go on and on about this machine....and I just got it yesterday!

reviewText: We have considered getting an ice maker for a few years and finally decided we were done dealing with making our own ice in the trays.  I love this ice maker.  Produces a great amount in a short period of time.  It is great for drinks and I love using the ice with my magic bullet as the cubes are small enough they don't damage the blades.  There were some reviews that state it was loud.  I didn't find it much louder then a refrigerator ice maker where you can hear the water running/getting pulled in, hear the ice drop into the tray.  We use it every day and now wonder why we waited so long to get one.

reviewText: After moving from a home with an under the counter dishwasher, to a home without a dishwasher, and no under the counter space to put one, it didn't take very long handwashing dishes before I had to find a solution.  I searched high and low for a "portable" dishwasher, and finally came across Danby.  I ordered it, waited for delivery, and have now been using it for about 2 months.  I have to say this thing cleans BETTER than my under the counter dishwasher at my old house.  Because it is portable, I can even use it as a kitchen island!  Two of the lower wire racks fold down so you can clean large pots, pans, etc.  I would recommend this to anyone with no space to put an under the counter dishwasher.  Only precaution....be careful attaching the hose to your faucet.  If you push the connector too far down, the ball bearings will come out, and they are not easy to get back in place.

reviewText: We bought a Bertazzoni 48" gas range and installed it at our cabin this summer. It is beautiful. However, at my other house I have an AGA range with electric convection ovens and a gas cook top. LOVE IT! The problems I've encountered with the Bertazzoni are 1-no way of telling when the ovens are preheated, 2-no timer on the stove, 3-the exhaust fan that blows hot air into the kitchen is SO HOT...not so good for a summer cabin. However, the griddle is a star, the cook top works wonderfully and we have no complaints with that. I bought 2 oven thermometers so I can watch the ovens temps....but really that shouldn't be necessary. Sure don't need that on my AGA. This range is taking some getting used to but we're having fun whining about it and learning to make it work!

reviewText: This unit arrived in good order and quickly. I only wished a better models looked this good, By better, I mean that the light switch doesn't work properly and i need to get  new switch. It groans when I initially turn on the unit when it goes from high setting to low setting. I need to start it on low and slowly bring it up to the desired speed and on high, it might take a couple of minurs to settle down then it might take a couple of adjustmens for unit to find it's "groove." After that, and after the light switch finds a spot to settle in on the low setting, I can cook without irritation. Also, it is smaller in the rear of the unit than the front, but i don't find that much of a problem. I also had to take the little black grill off the front and although it has a small plastic solid shield, it still leaked a lot out the front of this vented installation, so I used black Gorilla Tape over the hole, replaced the grill and trimmed the tape. The light fixture doesn't accomodate bulbs any bigger than the 40w A15 bulbs, although I found LED bulbs that fit and use only 14 total watts rather than 80 watts suggested. I thought Broan used engineers to design these things. Both bulbs light up together instead of going from one bulb to two bulbs.

reviewText: I wish I could say we've had no issues, but, alas, we have. First, the top was warped when it finally arrived (yeah, it got here waaay later than it was supposed to, but that's on the Shipper and the Seller). Once that was replaced (2 months later), we could finally use it. Just 5 or 6 weeks later, we had a spring storm come through which knocked out power for an hour or so. Once we got power back, one of the burner indicator lights would stay on (when burner was clearly Off). After another really long 3 months or so, they finally arranged for a repairman to come. Lord, I wish that on no one.  The oven is great when everything is actually working properly. Buy the warranty! You're likely to need it.

reviewText:
The filter that came with my Samsung refrigerator functioned great for over 6 months, I bought a second one at Lowes for far too much and it worked great for another 6 months.  I then purchased two from Amazon for a great price, but each of them slowed to a trickle (45 seconds to fill a glass of water) within a month.  I tried going back to my local box store expensive filter and it has been going strong for 4 months now.

I'm not sure if Amazon got a bad batch or if they get an inferior quality product, but beware.

reviewText: I am writing this review of LG products so that consumers can be aware of the type of company they are dealing with.  I own an 8 year old high-end clothes dryer that cost over $1,200 when it was new.  The dryer worked great but then it broke down and it needs a part and I cannot get this part anywhere.  I was told by LGs management that LG stops producing replacement parts after just 7 years.  Now I have a big beautiful dryer that will not work.  If you are purchasing an expensive appliance, don't let this happen to you!

reviewText:
The company, unfortunately, doesn't stand behind its product.

The biggest tri-ring burner got deformed by itself. We use this stove at home, normal household. We are not cooking in big amounts, just for the family of five (now four, and no long cooking processes at all).

The burner just bended pretty noticeable, and its damage from the heat. In my personal opinion these parts should last forever, and now we have a problem what just makes any owner upset any time you come to turn this thing on.

To have this feature in the range you pay premium, and in this case one of the selling points becomes a hate point. Disappointing 

Beware! This range, priced pretty high, comes only with 1(one) year manufacturer warranty.

Person who was answering my questions over the phone didn't even make an effort to figure out the reason and type of problem, didn't even offer a way to solve the problem if it is manufacturers defect (and clearly it is), or customers fault.

reviewText: Prior to getting this it was either line dry everything or hit the laundromat. One isn't conducive to apartment living, the other isn't practical with our crazy, hectic lifestyle. This has been a great solution. I haven't noticed too much of a jump on my electric bill, and I do all the laundry for a household of 4 people, including a messy preschooler and an infant who seems to go through about 4-5 outfits a day. I tend to do 1-2 loads a day on average. Each load takes about 3 hours to dry, longer for heavy items like jeans or towels, shorter for lighter items. it isn't too heavy or too big, we have it sitting atop an old end table in a corner of our master bedroom with Dundas Jafine TDIDVKZW ProFlex Indoor Dryer Vent Kit with 4-Inch by 5-Foot ProFlex Duct resting on top.

reviewText:
I purchased these as a replacement for the overly expensive Samsung brand filters.  I had used Home Depot brand filters in the past with good success, but they were out of stock, so I thought these should be a safe bet.  I installed the filter one evening and the next morning  the tray under the filter was half filled with water.  I removed this one and installed the second filter.  Next morning, same results.  Back to Home Depot to purchase the Samsung brand filter, but, before installing I decided to take some measurements.  I measured the outside diameter of the "O" rings with a micrometer and got the following results:
Samsung - 1.100"
Pureline #1 - 1.089"
Pureline #2 - 1.083"
As you can see, the Pureline filter seals were between 11 and 17 thousandths of an inch undersized which explains the leakage.  If I had the time and inclination I possibly could have found some domestic "O" ring replacements that would have provided a satisfactory seal but it was much easier to just return them.  I note from reading the results that some people got good results from these filters but that certainly was not my case.

reviewText:
This little (or I guess it's a bit big compared to most people's USB microphones) guy does a great job.  I get very little background noise.  Even though it sits right next to my tower, which makes very audible fan noise, it barely picks it up.  On the other hand, it picks up everything I say perfectly, no matter whether I'm speaking in a normal voice, whispering, or shouting.  I've also streamed video games while using it, with the sound coming through the TV's speakers as opposed to through a mixer in my computer, and it did surprisingly well with that.  I couldn't be more pleased with my purchase.

I wonder if I really needed this one as opposed to the cheaper Snowball iCE, since I don't really need the multidirectional functions, but that's no real fault of the microphone.  You can switch off the multidirectional functions completely to prevent the mic from picking up unnecessary sound.

If you want more moisture in the room, these just don't do it.

reviewText:
Fits my dryer perfectly.  This is the second time I have changed this part.  After a time the seal wears and clothes get caught between the seal and the drum causing brown stains to our clothes.

This time when I changed the seal I realized that the paint on the dryer where the seal rubs against was worn away and was down to bare metal.  The metal had rust on it and was getting on the seal causing stains.  After changing the seal I also repainted the dryer with high temp white spray paint...so far so good...no stains.

reviewText:
I wouldn't suggest this to anyone, unless if you guys want to waste GAS. There is a design issue. The unit by itself looks very attractive with LED lights on the knobs, looks decent for the money. Writing this review after a month of use. I'm coming from an electric cooktop to GAS and we all know what to expect on which one cooks faster. Cooking on this one is really really slow when compared to electric.

My first issue with this one is having 5 burners in 30". Wife wanted one, I knew already that 5 on 30" is not going to help, having 5 burners on 36" makes lot of sense. Space is damn packed and if you cook on the central one then you can't use the side due to space issue. So, if you guys are buying...just because you or your wife wanted 5 on 30" then please drop the idea. Stick to 4 burners on  30" and you can use more than one burner at a time.

My second issue which is a stupid design issue, I see a lot of gap between the bottom of the vessel and the flame and so its taking a lot of time to heat the stuff. On the side burners its taking more than 15 mts to get small pot of water to boiling temperature, worst than electric. The grates are heavy which is a good thing, but they are elevated high and so giving more space between the bottom of the vessel and to the flame and there by wasting more gas to heat the vessel and the food. I use to cook pasta for my kids in 20+ mts and now for the same amount of pasta this one is taking 35+ mts.

I wouldn't recommend this to anyone after using it for one month. GE should fire the person who designed burner GRATES and the quality check team.

UPDATE: Uploading the pics...Save your $$$ and invest in a nice product with good design. Look at the gap between the flame and the vessel and think of how much gas is getting wasted.

reviewText:
Appears to work well (just installed today). I saw other reviews for this and similar parts indicating that the part received was not genuine GE. I ordered directly from Amazon and the part came in a sealed GE branded box (photo). It included wire insulation which at least one other reviewer said their part was missing.

The mounting screws are tricky - they have a split shaft and one formed a collar at the tip that made it very difficult to remove. Ended up removing the burner that the igniter is attached to in order to get a grip and to pry while unscrewing. In hindsight I would have removed that burner immediately as it made working with the old and new igniter much easier. I was able to get both old screws back on with the new igniter but would prefer to have had new screws just in case.

I wish I had extra fiber insulation to stuff behind the igniter after replacing the igniter and wires. This process seems to push the insulation back and away from the heat source.

i wish i had extra fiber insulation to stuff behind the igniter after replacing the igniter and wires. this process seems to push the insulation back and away from the heat source.

reviewText: This is a great product!  I moved into my parents home and the dishwasher rack had several nickel size places where the rubber coating had worn through and the rack was rusting.  About a dozen of the prongs that you set the plates between had also worn off and rusted at the top.  I did have to cut some of the prongs off about a half inch before the little rubber tips would go onto them.  The brush on coating for the rusted spots took only two coats.  The rack looks like new and I am very pleased with the product.  I would recommend it to anyone!

reviewText:
My fridge had stopped cooling, and began to make a loud click sound approximately every 5 minutes, preceded by a few seconds of a wind up buzzing sound that ended after the click. After doing some research, I found out that the likely culprit was the start relay.

Opened up my fridge, and removed the old start relay, which had part number 8EA14C3 (3 long pins with connections, and a small unconnected metal stub where the 4th pin would have been). After some googling, I found out that a 2262181 part is just another id number for the same 8EA14C3 (and other relays with similar ids all similarly have an analagous Whirlpool number), so no point in ordering the more expensive and harder to find 8EA14C3.

Unlike other commenters, my relay didn't have a rattling sound when shook. However, if you squeeze the pins on the sides of the relay, you should be able to open up the relay. After opening, and upon removing the disc held inside, I saw two scorch marks on one side where the metal pins made contact, confirming that the relay was in fact damaged. Likewise, for those that hear rattling sounds, it's an indicator that the internal disc is shattered (fragments from mine broke off after lightly touching the scorched areas).

While waiting for the replacement part to arrive, I re-inserted the disc so that the metal pins only made contact with the unburnt (white) areas (despite the chipped edges), and I was able to startup the fridge again. However, as I don't know of any potential long term damage or risks that come with using a faulty component, I wouldn't recommend this as a long term solution. But it might come in handy to preserve some of your food until the new part arrives.

reviewText:
We own this dishwasher and are completely happy with it.
We have had GE, Whirlpool and Bosch before but this is by far the best one yet.
No matter what you put in it will get clean! Please note that the reason we bought this machine is the fact that it has a build in water softener and when you live in an area with hard water this feature is essential. Our dishes used to be really dirty and not clean but with Miele everything gets sparkling clean without this typical hard feel to glassware.
I would buy this machine again in a heartbeat.

reviewText: We bought this kegerator after living in Germany.  It is hard to drink American beer after living in Germany.  And, German beer is absurdly expensive by the bottle in the USA compared to how CHEAP it is across the pond.  So this was our Christmas gift to ourselves.  We decided on this model because we did not want to spend more than was necessary.  Of course, we had to purchase an alpha coupler for the German barrels, which added to the expense.  Our first keg was a Paulaner hefeweisen and it is a little taller so it BARELY fit into the fridge.  We had to do some strategic bending of the coupler....  and we had to remove the extra tray for barrel support.  but we made it work.  Our second keg is a Franziskaner and it still would have required bending the neck and removing the barrel base.  So, this model is not ideal for those who want to use German kegs.  That said, it IS working, it WAS a great deal, and we ARE very happy with it.  The beer is cold.  It is not the quietest thing ever, however, it is not bad and it doesn't have to run that often.  It arrived with some minor quality issues that only effect the appearance.  But you get what you pay for and we recognize that.

reviewText: I'm quite pleased with this lil dishwasher.  It sometimes requires some creative stacking but works quite well.  If you're looking to pull dry dishes out of it at the end of the cycle you won't be happy but it's not an issue for me.  My only issue, and this is nothing to do with the dishwasher but may be pertinent to someone, was the connection.  I have one of those goose neck faucets on my sink and finding a connector to fit to the quick connect wasn't possible, at least not for the one I have.  Apparently it's not one of the standard sizes.  For most goose neck faucets it's not so much of a problem if you have a good ACE Hardware in town but no joy for me, had to find an alternate way to do it cause sending it back wasn't an option. reviewText: I've owned this stove for a few years now.  I love the fact it has 5 burners - BUT - the sparkers are always on the fritz and I have replaced BOTH oven ignitors.  The stainless on the stove is heavy duty, but the guts of this stove for this price - should last a lot longer.  Fischer Pakel customer service is frankly in two words - A JOKE!  They shouldn't even call it "customer service" because there is none.  I own their over priced BBQ and their two drawer dishwasher.  The dishwasher is the only thing that still works decently.  The BBQ has a lifetime warrant on the burners - but they are rusted and I have tried twice to get them to replace them - but unless they are completely rusted through and DANGEROUS - they refuse to replace them.  Every time I have called their authorized techs - they charge me a whopping service charge and do very little for it.  The cost of repairs on these units is outrageous.  Would I ever buy or recommend Fisher Pakel products to a freind?? NOT!
